name: eyebrow
text: AMD Developer Hackathon Track 2
textPreview: AMD Developer Hackathon Track 2

name: title
text: ClipForger-AMD turns one clip into accurate captions and four styles.
textPreview: ClipForger-AMD turns one clip into accurate captions and four styles.

name: deck-sub
text: A Fireworks-first, Gemma-ready video captioning pipeline with Groq fallback for reliability, designed for the Track 2 evaluator and packaged as a public linux/amd64 Docker image.
textPreview: A Fireworks-first, Gemma-ready video captioning pipeline with Groq fallback for reliability, designed for the Track 2 evaluator and packa...

name: callout
text: Submission-ready in one sentence: upload, caption, judge, export.
textPreview: Submission-ready in one sentence: upload, caption, judge, export.

name: callout2
text: The front end is for the demo. The Docker container is what the judges run.
textPreview: The front end is for the demo. The Docker container is what the judges run.

name: title
text: Why the split matters
textPreview: Why the split matters

name: lede
text: Captions stay literal and readable. Summaries carry the humor, but only after the evidence is grounded.
textPreview: Captions stay literal and readable. Summaries carry the humor, but only after the evidence is grounded.

name: left-head
text: What users get
textPreview: What users get

name: left-body
text:
• Burned-in captioned MP4
• SRT, VTT, TXT export
• Editable factual base
• Four distinct summary styles
• Submission JSON for the judge
textPreview: • Burned-in captioned MP4 | • SRT, VTT, TXT export | • Editable factual base | • Four distinct summary styles | • Submission JSON for the...

name: right-head
text: What the judge sees
textPreview: What the judge sees

name: right-body
text: The evaluator gets unseen video clips, not cached answers. Every output has to work on fresh content, so the pipeline must actually understand the clip and the requested style.
textPreview: The evaluator gets unseen video clips, not cached answers. Every output has to work on fresh content, so the pipeline must actually under...

name: note
text: That separation is the whole product: accessibility first, creativity second.
textPreview: That separation is the whole product: accessibility first, creativity second.

title: The demo looks polished because the hardest pieces are already solved

text: Each style is editable and saved back into the job output.

name: right
text: That gives judges a clean review path and a stronger story.

name: title
text: What makes this submission competitive
textPreview: What makes this submission competitive

name: body
text:
• Clear separation between captions and summaries
• Track 2 Docker entrypoint that matches the evaluator
• Fireworks-first routing with Groq fallback for reliability
• Grounded outputs that stay tied to transcript and visual context
• Real demo assets, export flow, and review checklist
• Public GitHub repo plus public linux/amd64 Docker image
